mnemonics.title: Phong Components
mnemonics.content:
A - Always (Ambient)
D - Do (Diffuse)
S - See Specular highlights

mnemonics.title: Phong Vector Names
mnemonics.content:
L - Light direction
N - Normal (surface)
R - Reflected ray
V - Viewer's eye

mnemonics.title: Phong Equation Parts
mnemonics.content: I = Keep (Ka) Asking (Ia) + Keep (Kd) Doing (Id) + Keep (Ks) Seeing (Is)

mnemonics.title: Shininess Effect
mnemonics.content:
n - Narrow highlight for shiny (high n)
n - Not narrow for rough (low n)

acronyms.usage: Used in diffuse calculation L·N and in computing reflection R
Fundamental to all three components - defines surface orientation
Used in specular component R·V calculation
Determines viewing angle for specular reflection
Multiplies ambient light intensity Ia in Phong equation
Multiplies diffuse light intensity Id in Phong equation
Multiplies specular light intensity Is in Phong equation
Controls specular highlight falloff - higher = sharper highlight

visualTips.visualization: Picture three separate lights: one gray omnipresent (ambient), one straight-on colored (diffuse), one angled mirror-like (specular). Sum all three contributions.
Think of L·N as how directly light hits surface. Max = 1 when light hits perpendicularly, 0 when light is parallel to surface (grazing).
R·V = 1 when viewer sees perfect mirror reflection (highlight). Drops to 0 when viewing from 90° away.
Think of spotlight beam width. High n = narrow beam (small highlight), Low n = wide beam (large highlight).

analogies.analogy: Like overall room brightness - light that seems to come from everywhere and nowhere, allowing you to see shapes even without direct light.
Like sunlight hitting a matte white wall - light scatters equally in all directions, brightness depends on angle.
Like a mirror or polished car - creates bright glint when viewed from right angle, only at specific spot.
Like wet vs dry sand - wet sand (high n) creates small sharp glints, dry sand (low n) creates soft diffuse shine.

analogies.concept: Phong as sum of three effects
analogies.analogy: Like mixing three paint layers: base gray coat (ambient), main color coat (diffuse), glossy varnish coat (specular).
